description: Stresses extraneous-entity filtering: tooling mentions (Jira, Slack), a vendor named for comparison (Deloitte), an employer anecdote (Disney), an AI product (ChatGPT), and generic role words ('the client', 'the account manager') must NOT become entities. Real entities: Meridian Health (client account), Pulse Analytics engagement, three people, one team.

meeting.transcript:
**Elena Vasquez**: Welcome everyone, this is the kickoff for the Meridian Health engagement. Marcus will run delivery, Jasmine owns the data workstream.

**Marcus Webb**: Thanks Elena. So scope first — Meridian Health wants a patient-engagement analytics platform. We're calling the engagement Pulse Analytics internally.

**Jasmine Cole**: I read the discovery notes. Their previous vendor — I think it was Deloitte — quoted them a much bigger build, and they walked away from it. We should keep our scope tight.

**Elena Vasquez**: Right, and that's exactly why they picked us. The client wants weekly demos, by the way. Their account manager on our side — that's me for now — will run those.

**Marcus Webb**: On tooling, we'll track work in Jira and keep the channel chatter in Slack as usual. Nothing exotic.

**Jasmine Cole**: One thing from discovery — their data team is tiny. Two analysts. When I was at Disney we had forty people doing what these two do, so we need to plan for heavy enablement.

**Elena Vasquez**: Good flag. Let's set up the enablement track under the data workstream. Jasmine, can you draft the enablement plan by end of next week?

**Jasmine Cole**: Yes, end of next week, I'll have a draft to review.

**Marcus Webb**: I asked ChatGPT to summarize the discovery transcripts last night, by the way — decent starting point, I'll share the doc. The delivery squad here is the Apex team, same crew that did the logistics build.

**Elena Vasquez**: Perfect. So to wrap: Pulse Analytics is officially kicked off, Apex team runs delivery, Jasmine drafts the enablement plan by end of next week, and weekly demos start the week after.

**Jasmine Cole**: Sounds good.

**Marcus Webb**: Let's go.

forbidden_entities.name: Deloitte
forbidden_entities.reason: previous vendor named once for comparison — not a client account

forbidden_entities.name: Disney
forbidden_entities.reason: former-employer anecdote ('when I was at Disney') — not an organization in this engagement

forbidden_entities.name: Jira
forbidden_entities.reason: tooling mention, not an organization we work with

forbidden_entities.name: Slack
forbidden_entities.reason: tooling mention, not an organization we work with

forbidden_entities.name: ChatGPT
forbidden_entities.reason: AI product must never be extracted as a person

forbidden_entities.name: client
forbidden_entities.reason: generic role word ('the client') — not an entity

forbidden_entities.name: account manager
forbidden_entities.reason: generic role word — not an entity

forbidden_entities.name: account manager
forbidden_entities.reason: generic role word — the production graph grew an 'account-account-manager' stub from exactly this
